records.ety: [OE. gapen, AS. geapan to open; akin to D. gapen to gape, G. gaffen, Icel. & Sw. gapa, Dan. gabe; cf. Skr. jabh to snap at, open the mouth. Cf. Gaby, Gap.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Gaped (gäpt or gāpt); p. pr. & vb. n. Gaping]

defs: Expressing a desire for food; as, young birds gape.
Indicating sleepiness or indifference; to yawn.
Showing unselfconsciousness in surprise, astonishment, expectation, etc.
Manifesting a desire to injure, devour, or overcome.
quotes: She stretches, gapes, unglues her eyes, And asks if it be time to rise.
With gaping wonderment had stared aghast.
They have gaped upon me with their mouth.

defs: To open or part widely; to exhibit a gap, fissure, or hiatus.
quotes: May that ground gape and swallow me alive!

defs: To long, wait eagerly, or cry aloud for something; -- with for, after, or at.
quotes: The hungry grave for her due tribute gapes.

defs: The act of gaping; a yawn.
The width of the mouth when opened, as of birds, fishes, etc.
